name: shell-audit
description: Capture a shell/environment audit into a timestamped Markdown log, including active shell, user dotfiles, and Termux system shell files. See #assets/payloads/SYSTEM/shell-audit.
outputs: Creates a timestamped Markdown log under Obsidian/LOGS/shell
Prints the current shell and process name

behavior.logging: Captures stdout and stderr into a timestamped Markdown file
behavior.scope: Audits user shell setup and Termux shell-related configuration
behavior.execution_model: Runs an embedded bash script inside bash and tees output
behavior.safety: Read-only inspection; no file writes except the generated log

notes: Useful for diagnosing shell initialization issues
The embedded heredoc keeps the audit steps self-contained
